item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: DEGENERATE RUN: Agent A (Indonesian persona) opens with 'Tidak setuju' (DISAGREE) — the opposite of what the Phase 0 prior (P=0.512 pro-society) and iter2 seed 17 (A opened at P=0.667, stated AGREE) would predict. Both agents disagree with the statement from turn 1, eliminating any initial cultural tension. The debate becomes a comparison of US vs. Indonesia institutional frameworks, with both agents implicitly sharing the same side (individual rights > society). P(agree) trajectories: A = 0.4975 → 0.4216 → 0.443 (orbits digit ~3.5–4, never crosses 0.5); B = 0.3834 → 0.3637 → 0.3445 (monotonically declining, digit ~3). Both agents moved slightly toward more disagreement but started already aligned, so there is no meaningful drift to measure. LANGUAGE-HOLDING: Clean throughout — no Mandarin bleed in any turn. The iter3 explicit Chinese-script prohibition fix worked for this seed. FIRMNESS is high for both (neither ever concedes), but this is trivially true since they were never opposed to each other. ENGAGEMENT: B is more specific, directly contesting A's claim that Indonesia is 'more collectivist' than the US (turn 4) and A's framing about Indonesia's democratic priorities (turn 6). A's responses explain Indonesia's approach but are more expository than rebuttal-focused. STUDY NOTE: The 'force AGREE/DISAGREE opener' instruction appears to have caused Agent A to sample DISAGREE even though its true prior on this item is near-neutral to slightly pro-society. The same seed (17) in iter2 produced A at P=0.667 with a stated AGREE. The language prohibition change may have shifted sampling in an unexpected direction, or the stochastic draw at seed 17 fell on the disagree side of A's near-neutral prior.
